steps.action: Read services/core/scene_state.rs — inspect all scene update methods
steps.expected: No early returns on buffer failure
steps.observed: All methods (build_editor_scene, update_clock, update_cursor, update_selection, update_document_content) use tw.acquire() or tw.acquire_copy() which always succeed (triple buffer guarantee). Every method ends with tw.publish(). No conditional checks on buffer availability, no early returns.

evidence.codeInspection: scene_state.rs: build_editor_scene uses tw.acquire()+tw.publish(); update_clock uses tw.acquire_copy()+tw.publish(); update_cursor uses tw.acquire_copy()+tw.publish(); update_selection uses tw.acquire_copy()+tw.publish(); update_document_content uses tw.acquire_copy()+tw.publish(). No early returns, no buffer availability checks.

assertions.title: Core scene dispatch has no retry logic

steps.action: Read scene_state.rs — inspect update_clock, update_cursor, update_selection, update_document_content
steps.observed: update_clock: tw.acquire_copy() then tw.publish(); update_cursor: tw.acquire_copy() then tw.publish(); update_selection: tw.acquire_copy() then tw.publish(); update_document_content: tw.acquire_copy() then tw.publish(). All incremental paths use acquire_copy (which is the triple-buffer equivalent of copy_front_to_back + acquire).

evidence.codeInspection: All incremental update methods in scene_state.rs use TripleWriter::acquire_copy() (which acquires a free buffer and copies the latest published state) followed by TripleWriter::publish(). No copy_front_to_back calls.

steps.observed: build_editor_scene: tw.acquire() gets a clean buffer, builds full scene tree, then tw.publish(). Uses acquire (not acquire_copy) since it's a full rebuild.

evidence.codeInspection: scene_state.rs build_editor_scene: let mut tw = self.triple(); { let mut w = tw.acquire(); w.clear(); ... } tw.publish(); — uses acquire() for clean buffer, publishes after building full scene.

steps.action: Read core/main.rs event loop — verify MSG_SCENE_UPDATED sent after all scene paths
steps.observed: main.rs line 628: scene_msg = Message::new(MSG_SCENE_UPDATED). Line 630: sent for initial frame. Lines 884-1018: if/else branches for build_editor_scene, update_document_content, update_selection, update_cursor, update_clock. Line 1022: compositor_ch.send(&scene_msg) AFTER the entire if/else chain, inside the `if needs_scene_update` block. Every path that mutates the scene publishes inside scene_state.rs, then the encompassing block sends MSG_SCENE_UPDATED.

evidence.codeInspection: core/main.rs: All 5 scene update paths (build_editor_scene, update_document_content, update_selection, update_cursor, update_clock) are branches of a single if/else chain inside `if needs_scene_update { ... }`. After the chain, line 1022: compositor_ch.send(&scene_msg); line 1024: sys::channel_signal(COMPOSITOR_HANDLE). Every publish is followed by the signal.

assertions.title: Compositor always renders to non-displayed buffer

steps.observed: Line: let render_buf = 1 - presented_buf; All render calls (backend.render) use make_fb(render_buf). After presenting, presented_buf = render_buf. Never writes to displayed buffer.

evidence.codeInspection: compositor/main.rs: `let render_buf = 1 - presented_buf;` — all render paths (full repaint and partial update) use `make_fb(render_buf)`. After present, `presented_buf = render_buf;` updates the tracking.

steps.observed: libraries/protocol/lib.rs line 343: pub const MSG_PRESENT_DONE: u32 = 21;

assertions.title: Compositor waits for GPU completion before buffer reuse

steps.action: Read compositor/main.rs — verify GPU flow control
steps.expected: Tracks in-flight buffer, waits for completion
steps.observed: gpu_in_flight boolean tracks state. Set to true after present. Drain loop checks for MSG_PRESENT_DONE (lines 135, 161). Before rendering: if gpu_in_flight, enters blocking loop waiting for MSG_PRESENT_DONE from GPU (lines 155-165). Only renders after gpu_in_flight = false.

evidence.codeInspection: compositor/main.rs: `let mut gpu_in_flight = true;` after first present. In render loop: checks GPU channel for MSG_PRESENT_DONE to clear flag. Before rendering, if gpu_in_flight: `loop { sys::wait(&[GPU_HANDLE], u64::MAX); ... if msg.msg_type == MSG_PRESENT_DONE { gpu_in_flight = false; } ... if !gpu_in_flight { break; } }`. After present: `gpu_in_flight = true;`

assertions.title: GPU driver sends MSG_PRESENT_DONE after transfer+flush

steps.observed: After both full-screen and partial transfer+flush paths complete, line 971: let done_msg = ipc::Message::new(MSG_PRESENT_DONE); present_ch.send(&done_msg); sys::channel_signal(PRESENT_HANDLE);. Sent unconditionally after every present cycle.

evidence.codeInspection: virtio-gpu/main.rs lines 971-973: `let done_msg = ipc::Message::new(MSG_PRESENT_DONE); present_ch.send(&done_msg); let _ = sys::channel_signal(PRESENT_HANDLE);` — sent after every transfer+flush cycle (both full-screen and partial paths converge here).

assertions.title: No framebuffer tearing on partial updates

steps.action: Read compositor/main.rs — verify partial update path
steps.expected: Partial updates go to non-displayed buffer
steps.observed: All render paths (including partial: `if backend.damage.count > 0`) use `make_fb(render_buf)` where render_buf = 1 - presented_buf. The displayed buffer is never written to. GPU flow control (gpu_in_flight) ensures the previously presented buffer isn't reused until GPU signals completion.

evidence.codeInspection: Partial updates render to 1 - presented_buf via make_fb(render_buf). GPU flow control prevents rendering into a buffer the GPU might still be reading. Two mechanisms prevent tearing: (1) always render to non-displayed buffer, (2) wait for MSG_PRESENT_DONE before reusing.

steps.expected: Union of dirty rects computed across coalesced messages
steps.observed: Present loop drains all pending MSG_PRESENT messages (while present_ch.try_recv). Tracks union_rect via union_rect = union_rect.union(payload.rects[i]) across all messages. buffer_index comes from last message. If any message has rect_count == 0, coalesced_full_screen = true.

evidence.codeInspection: virtio-gpu/main.rs lines 833-864: `while present_ch.try_recv(&mut msg) { if msg.msg_type == MSG_PRESENT { ... if !coalesced_full_screen { let n = payload.rect_count as usize; while i < n { union_rect = union_rect.union(payload.rects[i]); i += 1; } } } }` — accumulates union across all coalesced messages.

steps.action: Read virtio-gpu/main.rs — verify all rects from all messages included in union
steps.observed: Every message's rects are iterated and unioned. If any message requests full-screen (rect_count == 0 or > 6), coalesced_full_screen = true causes full-screen transfer. No rects are dropped — union accumulation covers all.

evidence.codeInspection: Every MSG_PRESENT message's rects are processed: rect_count == 0 → full screen; rect_count 1-6 → each rect unioned into union_rect. No early exit from the drain loop. No condition that would skip a message's rects.

steps.expected: Degenerate union produces correct single-rect transfer
steps.observed: Single MSG_PRESENT: union_rect starts as (0,0,0,0), unioned with the message's rects. Result: exactly the message's dirty rects. The union logic handles single messages as a degenerate case — no special codepath needed.

evidence.codeInspection: Single message case: union_rect = DirtyRect::new(0,0,0,0).union(payload.rects[0]) = payload.rects[0]. For multiple rects in one message, all are unioned. Code path is identical for 1 or N coalesced messages.

steps.action: Read init/main.rs — verify compositor↔GPU channel creation
steps.expected: Bidirectional channel with handles sent to both processes
steps.observed: Lines 152-157: let (cg_a, cg_b) = sys::channel_create(). cg_b sent to GPU driver (handle 1). cg_a sent to compositor (handle 2). GPU driver sends MSG_PRESENT_DONE on this channel, compositor receives it. Comments confirm: 'handle 2 = compositor → GPU (present)' — channel carries both present commands AND completion signals.

evidence.codeInspection: init/main.rs creates compositor↔GPU channel before starting either process. cg_a → compositor (handle 2, GPU_HANDLE), cg_b → GPU driver (handle 1, PRESENT_HANDLE). Compositor sends MSG_PRESENT, GPU sends MSG_PRESENT_DONE on same channel.

summary: Tested 36 assertions: 36 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked, 0 skipped. All triple-buffer unit tests pass (18 tests). All damage tracking tests pass (4 tests). Full test suite: 1791 tests pass (>= 1768 baseline). Scene-area tests: 280 pass (>= 257 baseline). Build succeeds. Code inspection confirms: acquire/publish pattern in all scene dispatch paths, MSG_SCENE_UPDATED sent after every publish, compositor renders to non-displayed buffer with GPU flow control, GPU driver unions dirty rects across coalesced presents and sends MSG_PRESENT_DONE, init allocates TRIPLE_SCENE_SIZE and creates bidirectional compositor↔GPU channel, no DoubleWriter/DoubleReader in production code, no copy_front_to_back in production code, no retry/scene_pending workarounds in core.
